description: Design-system discipline: prefer tokens/components over hardcoded visual values so the UI stays consistent and themeable. All rules are WARN — a one-off literal is sometimes legitimate (a chart legend, a third-party embed), so these report rather than block. Tighten or redefine any rule id in the repo's .invariants.json once the team agrees it should be HARD, or narrow `include` to the component dirs. The DS component-substitution rules (prefer <Button> over <button>, etc.) are intentionally NOT here — the component names are repo-specific; add them as repo-local `rules`.

rules.message: hardcoded hex color on a style property — use a design token / theme variable so dark mode and rebrands work. Anchored to a color-ish property to avoid flagging unrelated hex (ids, keys).
fontSize in raw px — use a typography token or rem so the UI respects the user's font-scale settings.
Tailwind arbitrary value (e.g. text-[#fff], w-[437px]) bypasses the design scale — use a scale class (text-sm, w-96) or extend the theme/token config.
static inline style object — prefer className / design tokens for themeable, reusable styling. Dynamic styles (transform, computed dimensions, CSS custom properties) are not flagged.
